message.content:
You are reading the REAL POLARIS codebase at /home/polaris/wt/flywheel. Do not speculate — open the files and quote them.

Find how evidence rows are assigned to sections and handed to the writer (the "menu"/basket/ev_id assignment, global evidence assignment, topic judge dispositions). Report the exact structure of an evidence row (its fields) — we need to know what metadata exists per finding (technology? unit of analysis? outcome? method? industry? geography?) to build a comparison matrix, and what would have to be DERIVED because it is not stored.

Ground everything in file:line. If something does not exist, say so plainly — that is a finding.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

70	# Canonical tier order for the "Actual distribution" disclosure. ALL seven
71	# tiers are ALWAYS shown (including 0% ones) so the disclosure has no gap.
72	_DISCLOSURE_TIER_ORDER = ("T1", "T2", "T3", "T4", "T5", "T6", "T7")
73	
74	
75	def _tier_distribution_percentages(
76	    tier_counts: dict[str, int],
77	    total_sources: int,
78	) -> list[tuple[str, int]]:
79	    """Integer tier percentages that sum to EXACTLY 100 (largest-remainder).
80	
81	    Single source of truth = the raw ``tier_counts`` histogram over
82	    ``total_sources``. Every canonical tier T1..T7 is present in the result
83	    (0% when the corpus has none) plus any extra tier key found in
84	    ``tier_counts`` (e.g. ``UNKNOWN``) so no source is silently dropped from the
85	    denominator. Percentages are apportioned with the largest-remainder
86	    (Hamilton) method: floor each tier's exact share, then hand the leftover
87	    whole-percent points one-by-one to the tiers with the largest fractional
88	    remainders (deterministic tie-break: larger remainder first, then canonical
89	    order). This guarantees the printed percentages sum to 100 when
90	    ``total_sources > 0`` — replacing the per-tier independent ``round`` that
91	    could sum to 99 or 101 and silently omitted 0% tiers.
92	
93	    RENDER/DISCLOSURE TEXT ONLY — faithfulness-neutral. Touches no gate.
94	    """
95	    extra = sorted(k for k in tier_counts if k not in _DISCLOSURE_TIER_ORDER)
96	    tiers = list(_DISCLOSURE_TIER_ORDER) + extra
97	
98	    if total_sources <= 0:
99	        return [(t, 0) for t in tiers]
100	
101	    exact = {t: tier_counts.get(t, 0) * 100.0 / total_sources for t in tiers}
102	    floors = {t: int(exact[t]) for t in tiers}
103	    remainder_points = 100 - sum(floors.values())
104	    # Deterministic ranking of tiers by fractional remainder (desc), then order.
105	    ranked = sorted(
106	        tiers,
107	        key=lambda t: (-(exact[t] - floors[t]), tiers.index(t)),
108	    )
109	    result = dict(floors)
110	    for i in range(max(0, remainder_points)):
111	        result[ranked[i % len(ranked)]] += 1
112	    return [(t, result[t]) for t in tiers]

173	def run_honest_pipeline(
174	    *,
175	    research_question: str,
176	    domain: str,
177	    run_id: str,
178	    run_dir: Path | str,
179	    retrieved_sources: list[dict[str, Any]],
180	    evidence: list[dict[str, Any]],
181	    draft_text: str,
182	    approval_note: str = "",
183	    auto_approve_if_within_bounds: bool = True,
184	    quantified_models: dict[tuple[str, str], Any] | None = None,
185	) -> PipelineResult:
186	    """Run the honest-rebuild pipeline end-to-end in offline mode.
187	
188	    Args:
189	        research_question: User query (verbatim).
190	        domain: clinical / policy / tech / due_diligence.
191	        run_id: Stable identifier.
192	        run_dir: Directory for artifacts.
193	        retrieved_sources: List of dicts with fields used by
194	            tier_classifier (url / title / publisher / content_length /
195	            openalex_pub_type / openalex_source_type / etc.) plus 'domain'.
196	        evidence: List of evidence rows. Each dict has:
197	            evidence_id, source_url, statement, direct_quote, tier.
198	            (tier is populated by the tier classifier step below.)
199	        draft_text: Prose draft with [#ev:id:start-end] provenance tokens.
200	        approval_note: Explanation if material deviation from protocol.
201	        auto_approve_if_within_bounds: If True and no material
202	            deviation, approve automatically.
203	    """
204	    run_dir_path = Path(run_dir)
205	    run_dir_path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
206	
207	    # ── Phase 2b: scope gate ────────────────────────────────────────────
208	    scope_result = run_scope_gate(
209	        research_question=research_question,
210	        run_dir=run_dir_path,
211	        run_id=run_id,
212	        domain=domain,
213	    )
214	    protocol_dict = scope_result.protocol.to_json_dict()
215	
216	    # ── Phase 2a: tier classification of retrieved sources ─────────────
217	    classified_sources: list[CorpusSource] = []
218	    for src in retrieved_sources:
219	        signals = _build_tier_signals(src)
220	        res = classify_source_tier(signals)
221	        classified_sources.append(CorpusSource(
222	            url=src.get("url", ""),
223	            title=src.get("title", ""),
224	            domain=src.get("domain", ""),
225	            tier=res.tier.value,
226	            tier_confidence=res.confidence,
227	            tier_rule=res.matched_rules[0] if res.matched_rules else "",
228	            tier_reasons=list(res.reasons),
229	        ))
230	
231	    # Attach tier to each evidence row whose source_url matches
232	    url_to_tier = {c.url: c.tier for c in classified_sources}
233	    for ev in evidence:
234	        url = ev.get("source_url", "")
235	        if url in url_to_tier:
236	            ev["tier"] = url_to_tier[url]
237	
238	    # ── Phase 2g: corpus approval gate ──────────────────────────────────
239	    report = compute_tier_distribution(classified_sources, protocol_dict)
240	    approved = False
241	    # FX-05 (I-ready-017): a material-deviation corpus auto-approves ONLY with a
242	    # structured authorization (PG_AUTHORIZED_SWEEP_APPROVAL), never a free-text
243	    # note. `approval_note` is descriptive/audit only.
244	    authorization = authorization_from_env()
245	    if auto_approve_if_within_bounds and not report.has_material_deviation:
246	        approved = True
247	    else:
248	        ok, _ = check_auto_approve_allowed(report, authorization)
249	        approved = ok
250	
251	    decision = CorpusApprovalDecision(
252	        run_id=run_id,
253	        decision_at_unix=time.time(),
254	        decision_at_iso=time.strftime(
255	            "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", time.gmtime()
256	        ),
257	        approved=approved,
258	        user_note=approval_note,
259	        authorization=authorization,
260	        approved_source_urls=[s.url for s in classified_sources] if approved else [],
261	        rejected_source_urls=[] if approved else [s.url for s in classified_sources],
262	        report=report,
263	        protocol_sha256=scope_result.protocol_sha256,
264	    )
265	    corpus_approval_path = save_approval_decision(decision, run_dir_path)
266	
267	    # FX-05 (I-ready-017): §9.1 #5 — a denied corpus aborts BEFORE any
268	    # strict-verify / report / evaluator work. A material-deviation corpus with
269	    # no structured PG_AUTHORIZED_SWEEP_APPROVAL authorization is denied; emit a
270	    # pipeline-verdict report and return early (no normal report on a denied
271	    # corpus).
272	    if not approved:
273	        abort_report_path = run_dir_path / "report.md"
274	        abort_report_path.write_text(
275	            f"# Research report: {research_question}\n\n"
276	            "## Pipeline verdict\n\n"
277	            "Corpus approval was denied: the corpus has a material deviation "
278	            "from the pre-registered protocol and no structured operator "
279	            "authorization (PG_AUTHORIZED_SWEEP_APPROVAL=1) was supplied. "
280	            "No report was synthesized.\n\n"
281	            "Status: abort_corpus_approval_denied\n",
282	            encoding="utf-8",
283	        )
284	        abort_manifest_path = run_dir_path / "manifest.json"
285	        abort_manifest_path.write_text(
286	            json.dumps(
287	                {
288	                    "run_id": run_id,
289	                    "status": "abort_corpus_approval_denied",
290	                    "corpus_approved": False,
291	                },
292	                indent=2, sort_keys=True,
293	            ) + "\n",
294	            encoding="utf-8",
295	        )
296	        abort_artifacts = PipelineArtifacts(
297	            run_dir=run_dir_path,
298	            protocol_path=scope_result.protocol_path,
299	            corpus_approval_path=corpus_approval_path,
300	            contradictions_path=run_dir_path / "contradictions.json",
301	            report_path=abort_report_path,
302	            bibliography_path=run_dir_path / "bibliography.json",
303	            evaluator_output_path=run_dir_path / "evaluator_output.json",
304	            manifest_path=abort_manifest_path,
305	        )
306	        return PipelineResult(
307	            artifacts=abort_artifacts,
308	            scope_result=scope_result,
309	            corpus_decision=decision,
310	            contradictions_found=0,
311	            final_report_text="",
312	            evaluator=None,
313	            sentences_verified=0,
314	            sentences_dropped=0,
315	            status="abort_corpus_approval_denied",
316	        )

318	    # ── Phase 3: contradiction detection ───────────────────────────────
319	    # B9: route + label by the deterministic is_clinical signal so a non-clinical
320	    # run uses the domain-agnostic extractor and possible_metric_mismatch
321	    # labeling. Clinical (domain="clinical") is byte-identical.
322	    from src.polaris_graph.domain.domain_signal import is_clinical_domain
323	    _is_clinical_hp = is_clinical_domain(domain, evidence)
324	    num_claims = extract_numeric_claims(evidence, domain=domain)
325	    contradictions = detect_contradictions(num_claims, is_clinical=_is_clinical_hp)
326	    contradictions_path = run_dir_path / "contradictions.json"
327	    contradictions_path.write_text(

329	            [serialize_contradiction_record(c) for c in contradictions],

331	        ) + "\n",
332	        encoding="utf-8",
333	    )

335	    # ── Phase 4: strict verification of draft + resolution ─────────────
336	    # MOAT SEAM (2026-07-11): thread the agentic outliner's verified quantified-model
337	    # registry so a computed number rendered as a ``[#calc:model:hash:field]`` token is
338	    # force-routed to ``verify_modeled_atom`` (the Regime-C calc router) instead of being
339	    # dropped ``no_provenance_token``. Default None => byte-identical legacy behaviour
340	    # (the router is skipped, exactly as before). A derived number can NEVER launder through
341	    # the ``[#ev:]`` span path (``number_not_in_any_cited_span`` still fires), so this only
342	    # OPENS the verified compute lane in production — it never widens the render surface.
343	    evidence_pool = {ev["evidence_id"]: ev for ev in evidence}
344	    strict = strict_verify(draft_text, evidence_pool, quantified_models=quantified_models)
345	    rendered_text, biblio = resolve_provenance_to_citations(
346	        strict.kept_sentences, evidence_pool,
347	    )
348	
349	    report_path = run_dir_path / "report.md"
350	    biblio_path = run_dir_path / "bibliography.json"
351	
352	    # Augment rendered text with a methods section that includes the
353	    # PRISMA-trAIce disclosures Phase 5 looks for.
354	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import (
355	        PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL,
356	        PG_GENERATOR_MODEL,
357	    )
358	    retrieval_date = time.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
359	    methods_section = (
360	        "\n\n## Methods\n"
361	        f"This research follows a pre-registered protocol.json artifact.\n"
362	        f"Retrieved on {retrieval_date} from PubMed, OpenAlex, and Semantic Scholar.\n"
363	        f"Generator model: {PG_GENERATOR_MODEL}.\n"
364	        f"Evaluator model: {PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL} (different family).\n"
365	        f"Sources were classified using the T1-T7 tier taxonomy "
366	        f"(T1 peer-reviewed primary, T2 SR/MA, T3 regulatory, T4 "
367	        f"narrative review, T5 industry, T6 commentary/news, T7 "
368	        f"conference abstract / stub).\n"
369	        f"Inclusion criteria: peer-reviewed journal articles, regulatory\n"
370	        f"documents, human studies. Exclusion criteria: user-upload\n"
371	        f"document hosts, student journals, press releases without a\n"
372	        f"peer-reviewed primary source. Sponsor / conflict-of-interest\n"
373	        f"funding was evaluated per source.\n"
374	        f"Prompt-injection sanitization was applied to all evidence "
375	        f"before prompt construction.\n"
376	        f"Expected tier distribution per the clinical template: T1 "
377	        f"30-60%, T2 15-40%, T3 5-25%. Actual distribution: "
378	    )
379	    # Append actual tier distribution. Percentages are computed from the raw
380	    # tier_counts (single source of truth) with largest-remainder rounding so
381	    # they sum to EXACTLY 100%, and ALL tiers T1..T7 are shown (including 0%
382	    # ones) so there is no gap. Any extra tier key (e.g. UNKNOWN) is appended so
383	    # no source is dropped from the denominator.
384	    actual_parts = [
385	        f"{tier}={pct}%"
386	        for tier, pct in _tier_distribution_percentages(
387	            report.tier_counts, report.total_sources,
388	        )
389	    ]
390	    methods_section += ", ".join(actual_parts) + ".\n"
391	
392	    # Contradiction disclosure paragraph
393	    if contradictions:
394	        n = len(contradictions)
395	        pluralized = "contradiction" if n == 1 else "contradictions"
396	        verb = "was" if n == 1 else "were"
397	        methods_section += (

399	            f"{n} {pluralized} {verb} detected and {'is' if n == 1 else 'are'} "
400	            f"disclosed below alongside {'its' if n == 1 else 'their'} "
401	            f"source tiers.\n\n"
402	        )
403	        for c in contradictions:
404	            methods_section += (
405	                f"- {c.subject} / {c.predicate}: "
406	                f"values range {c.claims[0].value} to {c.claims[-1].value} "
407	                f"{c.claims[0].unit} "
408	                f"(relative difference {c.relative_difference*100:.1f}%). "
409	                f"Sources: "
410	                + ", ".join(
411	                    f"ev={cc.evidence_id} tier={cc.source_tier}"
412	                    for cc in c.claims
413	                )
414	                + ".\n"
415	            )
416	
417	    # Bibliography section
418	    biblio_section = "\n\n## Bibliography\n"
419	    for b in biblio:
420	        biblio_section += (
421	            f"[{b['num']}] {b['statement']} — {b['url']} (tier {b['tier']})\n"
422	        )
423	
424	    final_report_text = (
425	        f"# Research report: {research_question.strip()}\n\n"

427	        + methods_section

429	    )
430	
431	    report_path.write_text(final_report_text, encoding="utf-8")
432	    biblio_path.write_text(

449	        encoding="utf-8",
450	    )
451	
452	    # ── Manifest ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
453	    manifest_path = run_dir_path / "manifest.json"
454	    manifest = {
455	        "run_id": run_id,
456	        "domain": domain,

476	        },
477	    }
478	    manifest_path.write_text(
479	        json.dumps(manifest, indent=2, sort_keys=True) + "\n",

481	    )
482	
483	    artifacts = PipelineArtifacts(

486	        corpus_approval_path=corpus_approval_path,

496	        corpus_decision=decision,

def _render_map_user(
    *,
    section_title: str,
    section_focus: str,
    evidence_id: str,
    tier: str,
    statement: str,
    direct_quote: str,
    atom_rows: str,
    research_question: str = "",
) -> str:
    """Render the MAP user prompt (spec Â§3).

    I-arch-004 F21 (#1255): when `research_question` is supplied, a FRAMING-ONLY
    line is prepended so the per-source extractor can decide on-topic relevance
    against the actual question (the _MAP_SYSTEM rules already turn on
    "what THIS section is about"). It is NOT a citable source: findings are still
    extracted ONLY from DIRECT_QUOTE and validated against it. Empty (the default)
    => byte-identical to pre-F21."""
    _rq = (research_question or "").strip()
    rq_line = (
        "RESEARCH_QUESTION (framing only — extract findings ONLY from "
        f"DIRECT_QUOTE, never from this line): {_rq}\n"
        if _rq
        else ""
    )
    return (
        f"{rq_line}"
        f"SECTION_TITLE: {section_title}\n"
        f"SECTION_FOCUS: {section_focus}\n"
        f"EVIDENCE_ID: {evidence_id}\n"
        f"SOURCE_TIER: {tier}\n"
        f"STATEMENT: {statement}\n"
        f"DIRECT_QUOTE:\n"
        f"{direct_quote}\n\n"
        f"NUMERIC_ATOMS_AVAILABLE:\n"
        f"{atom_rows}\n\n"
        "Return JSON:\n"
        "{\n"
        '  "evidence_id": "...",\n'
        '  "no_relevant_findings": false,\n'
        '  "no_relevant_reason": "",\n'
        '  "findings": [\n'
        "    {\n"
        '      "claim": "single atomic claim",\n'
        '      "support_quote": "exact substring from DIRECT_QUOTE",\n'
        '      "span_start": 0,\n'
        '      "span_end": 0,\n'
        '      "numbers": [],\n'
        '      "entities": [],\n'
        '      "caveat": "",\n'
        '      "contradiction_key": "",\n'
        '      "source_tier": "T1"\n'
        "    }\n"
        "  ]\n"
        "}\n"
        "Rules: every number in claim must appear in support_quote; do not round; "
        "do not combine multiple claims; caveats/limitations are findings only when "
        "the quote directly states them.\n"
        "#1218 EXHAUSTIVE + ATOMIC: return a SEPARATE finding for EVERY distinct "

    )

def strict_verify(
    draft_text: str,
    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
    *,
    require_number_match: bool = True,
    telemetry_block: str | None = None,
    quantified_models: dict[tuple[str, str], Any] | None = None,
) -> StrictVerificationReport:
    """Run strict verification on a draft. Drops failing sentences.

    Gap-3 (original): the Limitations paragraph was pass-through because
    it discusses pipeline telemetry, not evidence.

    BUG-M-204 fix (deep-dive R10): when `telemetry_block` is supplied,
    Limitations sentences ARE verified against it — every numeric
    claim in the sentence must appear verbatim in the telemetry block.
    This catches a fabricated "only 3% of sources are T1" claim when
    the telemetry actually says "T1: 9%". Backward-compatible: if
    telemetry_block is None (default), falls back to pass-through.
    """
    findings_text, limitations_text = split_findings_and_limitations(draft_text)

    kept: list[SentenceVerification] = []
    dropped: list[SentenceVerification] = []

    # I-pipe-015 (#1240) + I-pipe-016 (#1241) flags, read once per call.
    _honest_tokens = _token_honest_drop_enabled()
    _skip_empty = _skip_empty_enabled()

    # I-pipe-016 (#1241): content-empty fragments are excluded from total_in
    # (denominator) — they are neither kept nor dropped, they do not count.
    _excluded_empty = 0

    # BUG-19 (I-arch-006, #1262): pre-gate web-crawl-chrome hygiene. Lazy-load the
    # allowlist-only helpers and strip confirmed crawl-marker LINES from the section
    # text BEFORE sentence-splitting, so a chrome line can't even become a candidate
    # "sentence" (the NTSB 404 "Page not found" hole, where boilerplate trivially
    # self-entails and is COUNTED as verified). `_is_boilerplate` then excludes any
    # surviving pure-chrome / error-page / pure-metadata unit from the gate's INPUT
    # below. INPUT hygiene only — no verdict logic / threshold / gate strictness
    # changes; every pattern is allowlist-only + whole-unit anchored so a real
    # clinical sentence (any language) is never touched. OFF-path (flag=0) is
    # byte-identical: helpers are never loaded, text is untouched.
    _pregate_boilerplate = _pregate_boilerplate_filter_enabled()
    _is_boilerplate = None
    if _pregate_boilerplate:
        _strip_web_boilerplate, _is_boilerplate = _load_boilerplate_helpers()
        findings_text = _strip_web_boilerplate(findings_text)
        limitations_text = _strip_web_boilerplate(limitations_text)

    # Findings: strict provenance verification
    findings_sentences = split_into_sentences(findings_text)
    # fix#19 (#1262): SEQUENTIAL pre-filter (cheap, deterministic) builds the
    # ORDERED list of canonicalized sentences that need the (expensive) per-sentence
    # judge. It owns _excluded_empty so the denominator semantics are unchanged —
    # only the per-sentence verify-or-rescue call (the blocking entailment judge) is
    # eligible to run in parallel below.
    _findings_to_verify: list[str] = []
    for s in findings_sentences:
        # I-pipe-015 (#1240): canonicalize a malformed `[ev:...]` token to
        # `[#ev:...]` so an otherwise-valid citation is not silently lost. The
        # canonical form is what flows to verify_sentence_provenance (and is
        # stored on .sentence), so downstream token-stripping in
        # resolve_provenance_to_citations works and no malformed literal leaks
        # into the rendered report. The canonicalized token STILL runs the full
        # validation — bracket format is fixed, no check is bypassed.
        if _honest_tokens:
            s = _canonicalize_malformed_ev_tokens(s)
        # I-pipe-016 (#1241): a content-empty fragment (orphaned citation /
        # punctuation residue with no content word and no number) is excluded
        # from BOTH numerator and denominator. verify_sentence_provenance would
        # already DROP it (BUG-03 / no_provenance_token), so excluding it from
        # the denominator instead of dropping it only removes empty noise from
        # the telemetry; it never lets an unverified REAL sentence through.
        if _skip_empty and _is_content_empty_sentence(s):
            _excluded_empty += 1
            continue
        # BUG-19 (#1262): a pure web-crawl-chrome / error-page-stub / bare-DOI /
        # table-number "sentence" carries NO assertional claim — it must NOT reach
        # the gate, where boilerplate would trivially self-entail and be COUNTED as
        # verified (the NTSB 404 "Page not found" hole). Exclude it from BOTH
        # numerator and denominator (same faithfulness-neutral treatment as the
        # content-empty path). INPUT hygiene only: the helper is allowlist-only +
        # whole-unit anchored, so a real clinical sentence is never flagged.
        if _is_boilerplate is not None and _is_boilerplate(s):
            _excluded_empty += 1
            continue
        _findings_to_verify.append(s)

3613	7. Do not write a section heading, section title, or preamble. Just the paragraph body.

3615	9. Citation diversity: cite at least 5 DISTINCT sources across this section (distinct ev_XXX IDs from different papers/URLs, not the same study cited five times). Every named trial, every numeric estimate, every guideline recommendation should be its own cited sentence.
3616	10. **Multi-source citation (M-27, for DR-grade citation density)**: When MULTIPLE evidence rows independently support the same claim, cite ALL of them, not just one. Example: "Tirzepatide reduced HbA1c 2.0-2.4% vs placebo across phase 3 trials [ev_012][ev_034][ev_055][ev_088]." rather than citing one ev_id. This raises citation density from ~1 per sentence to 2-4 per sentence where evidence supports it — top-tier DR (GPT-5.4 DR / Gemini 3.1 Pro DR) routinely reaches 50-200 citations for a clinical question by synthesizing multiple converging sources into each sentence, not by writing more sentences.
3617	11. **Jurisdictional precision (M-29, for multi-authority synthesis)**: When citing regulatory, standards-setting, or governance sources from more than one jurisdiction (different countries, agencies, courts, or rulemaking bodies), attribute every specific assertion to the ONE jurisdiction whose source supports it. Do NOT use generic plural language like "both agencies", "all regulators", "authorities generally", "regulators require", "jurisdictions mandate", or similar when the evidence you cite comes from a single jurisdiction. A boxed warning in one jurisdiction is not the same legal instrument as a precaution in another jurisdiction; a formal contraindication in one framework is not automatically equivalent to a warning in another. If the evidence supports only one jurisdiction's position, write: "Jurisdiction A's framework classifies X as a contraindication [ev_A]. Jurisdiction B's framework addresses X through warnings and precautions [ev_B]." 3618	11b. **Jurisdictional coverage (M-37, for multi-authority completeness)**: When this section's evidence subset contains sources from MULTIPLE regulatory jurisdictions (examples of distinct jurisdictions: US FDA, European EMA, UK NICE/MHRA, Health Canada, Australian TGA, Japanese PMDA, Chinese NMPA, WHO), you MUST cite at least ONE source from EACH jurisdiction whose content appears in your evidence subset. This rule fires only when a jurisdiction's evidence is actually present in your subset; it does not require you to invent coverage.
3619	12. **Primary-study framing (M-32, for claim-frame rigor)**: When you name a primary study, trial, cohort, experiment, or any individually identifiable empirical data source, and the cited evidence rows contain the structured metadata, provide the study's FULL FRAME in the FIRST sentence that introduces it: (a) sample size or cohort size (e.g. N=1879), (b) baseline value of the outcome being discussed (e.g. mean baseline [PRIMARY_METRIC]=[VALUE], baseline [SECONDARY_METRIC]=[VALUE]), (c) comparator / control / background condition (e.g. versus [COMPARATOR], versus placebo on [BACKGROUND], versus standard [REFERENCE_CONDITION]), and (d) the primary endpoint + timepoint (e.g. [ENDPOINT] change at [TIMEPOINT], [N-YEAR] [OUTCOME_TYPE], cycle-life to [THRESHOLD] retention). 543	    """PUSH 4 (S4-local title-like mitigation): True when a candidate basket claim is really a
544	    bare TITLE, not a claim sentence — an S3 representative_statement that fell back to the row

548	    — the upstream fix (S3 should emit a claim sentence) is filed as a separate note."""
549	    s = (statement or "").strip()

"""Deterministic, verified-only SUMMARY TABLE renderer (I-deepfix-001 P7, #1344).

Some research prompts explicitly ask the report to end with a summary TABLE and name
the exact column headers, e.g. drb_72:

    "... please create a summary table ... The table column headers should be
    "Research Literature", "Country/Region", "Application Area/Occupation",
    "Specific Applications and Impacts", and "Key Risks and Limitations"."

The multi-section generator produces span-verified narrative prose but does not emit
this requested GFM table, so ``report.md`` renders zero table rows and the DRB-II
"presentation" rubric fails on "article does not contain a summary table".

This module renders that table WITHOUT any LLM call, purely from ALREADY-verified
content, so it introduces NO new claim (CLAUDE.md §-1.3: a summary table is a
presentation of already-verified findings). Faithfulness contract:

* Every row is one CITED source that carries at least one strict-verify-passed claim.
* The "Research Literature" cell is bibliographic metadata (author / title / ``[N]``)
  drawn from the numbered bibliography — an identity, not a claim. Its source_title and
  authors are CHROME-SCREENED with the same predicate as the claim cell (a chrome /
  interstitial title or license/boilerplate author is dropped, the label falling back to a
  clean citation form) so no page furniture renders in the cell; the claim/row is kept.
* The "Specific Applications and Impacts" (claim-role) cell is the source's own
  verified sentence, verbatim (whitespace-normalised, excerpted with an ellipsis),
  carrying its ``[N]`` citation.
* The Country/Region, Application Area/Occupation and Key Risks cells surface ONLY
  short terms that appear VERBATIM in that source's verified spans (high-precision
  curated vocabularies). A cell with no verbatim match renders an em dash ``—`` — an
  honest DISCLOSED GAP; nothing is inferred or invented.

The faithfulness engine (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role D8 / provenance / span-grounding)
is UNTOUCHED — this reads the finished, verified bibliography + kept-sentence objects
and emits markdown. Behaviour is gated behind the default-ON kill-switch
``PG_RENDER_SUMMARY_TABLE`` (LAW VI); OFF => no table => byte-identical report.

        rep_id = b.get("representative_evidence_id")
        member_ids = list(b.get("member_evidence_ids") or [])

            dropped += 1

        clusters.append(SimpleNamespace(
            representative_index=ev_id_to_idx[rep_id],
            member_indices=[ev_id_to_idx[m] for m in member_ids],
            corroboration_count=int(b.get("corroboration_count", len(member_ids))),
            member_hosts=list(b.get("member_hosts") or []),
        ))

    return clusters

def register_outline_toolkit(
    registry: ToolRegistry,
    workspace: Any,
    agent_model: str,
    deadline: Optional[float] = None,
) -> list[str]:
    """Wire the T1 read-only/deterministic tools + verified_compute onto ``registry``. Returns the
    list of registered tool names. The driver iterates the registry unchanged."""

    def _bind(fn):
        async def _exec(evidence_store, data_points, client, **kw):  # noqa: ANN001
            return await fn(workspace, **kw)
        return _exec

    def _bind_am(fn):
        # fetch_url needs agent_model (for the fold-in topic judge); capture it in the closure so
        # the driver's uniform execute(...) contract is unchanged.
        async def _exec(evidence_store, data_points, client, **kw):  # noqa: ANN001
            return await fn(workspace, agent_model, **kw)
        return _exec

    specs = [
        ("fetch_url", _tool_fetch_url,
         "Targeted re-fetch of ONE named URL — turns 'source B cites source A' into actually "
         "reading A. Re-enters evidence through the SAME fold-in seam as search_more_evidence.",
         {"url": "the URL to fetch", "max_chars": "optional body cap (default 20000)"}),
        ("calculator", _tool_calculator,
         "Evaluate ONE arithmetic expression (deterministic, no LLM). EXPLORATORY — render a "
         "derived number via verified_compute, not this.",

        ("get_evidence", _tool_get_evidence,
         "Return the FULL text + metadata of one evidence row by id.",
         {"ev_id": "the evidence id", "max_chars": "optional text cap (default 4000)"}),
        ("search_corpus", _tool_search_corpus,
         "Keyword search over the evidence ALREADY in the pool (no network). The 'do we already "
         "have this?' stop before any live fetch.",
         {"query": "keywords to search", "top_k": "max hits (default 8)"}),
        ("list_baskets", _tool_list_baskets,
         "One line per basket (id, members, corroboration, assignment) — scan the whole corpus "
         "shape at once.",
         {}),
        ("coverage_audit", _tool_coverage_audit,
         "Deterministic coverage accounting: unassigned baskets, per-section counts, residual "
         "fraction, sections below floor.",
         {}),
        ("preview_section_evidence", _tool_preview_section_evidence,
         "Preview one section's assigned evidence — exactly what compose will receive.",
         {"section": "the section title"}),
        ("find_contradictions", _tool_find_contradictions,
         "Surface CONFLICT among evidence rows (opposite direction, or a magnitude outlier) — both "
         "sides cited, never averaged, never deleted. Deterministic, no network, no LLM.",
         {"ev_ids": "list of evidence ids to compare (default: all in the pool)",
          "outlier_ratio": "magnitude-outlier threshold (default 100)"}),
        ("corroboration_profile", _tool_corroboration_profile,
         "Per-basket corroboration at WORK level: distinct works (not row count), independent hosts, "
         "tier spread, and a SINGLE-WORK MASQUERADE flag when N rows collapse to one work. The 'is "
         "this claim really multi-source?' tool. Deterministic, no network, no LLM.",
         {"basket_id": "basket to profile in detail (default: scan all baskets, masquerades first)"}),
        ("verified_compute", _tool_verified_compute,
         "Derive a number through the VERIFIED lane and render it via a [#calc:] token (the ONLY "
         "compute output allowed to render).",
         {"question": "what is being computed",
          "datapoints": "list of sourced datapoints {evidence_id,label,context,value,unit}",
          "spec": "the model spec {model_id,title,inputs,outputs}",
          "render_field": "optional output field to render (default first)",
          "lead": "optional lead prose for the rendered sentence",
          "section": "outline section title this number belongs in — the composer appends the "
                     "verified sentence into that section body deterministically (default: "
                     "auto-home by the sourced datapoints' ev_ids)"}),
    ]
    _agent_model_tools = {"fetch_url"}
    # Decide-prompt scaling metadata (redesign "Scaling the decide step past 20 tools"): the CORE set
    # is always listed in full; the rest collapse into the categorized index past the threshold.
    _meta = {
        "fetch_url": (True, "retrieval"),
        "search_corpus": (True, "retrieval"),
        "get_evidence": (True, "retrieval"),
        "list_baskets": (False, "retrieval"),
        "calculator": (True, "compute"),
        "verified_compute": (True, "compute"),
        "coverage_audit": (True, "outline_ops"),
        "preview_section_evidence": (False, "outline_ops"),
        "find_contradictions": (False, "cross_source"),
        "corroboration_profile": (False, "cross_source"),
    }
    registered: list[str] = []
    for name, fn, desc, params in specs:

            parameters=params, execute=binder(fn), tags=[category], core=core,

import asyncio
import re
from typing import Any, Callable, Optional

from src.polaris_graph.tools.tool_registry import ToolDefinition, ToolRegistry, ToolResult

_WORD_RE = re.compile(r"[A-Za-z0-9]+")

# The evidence text fields, richest first (mirrors _fold_in / tradeoff_modeler ordering).
_TEXT_FIELDS = (
    "direct_quote", "statement", "full_text", "content", "extracted_text",
    "fetched_body", "raw_content", "raw_text", "page_text", "source_text", "body", "text",
)


def _row_text(row: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
    return max(
        (str(row.get(k) or "") for k in _TEXT_FIELDS),
        key=len, default="",
    )


def _tokens(text: str) -> list[str]:
    return _WORD_RE.findall(text.lower())


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# calculator — single deterministic arithmetic expression (EXPLORATORY lane)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
async def _tool_calculator(
    workspace: Any, *, expression: str = "", **_ignored: Any,
) -> ToolResult:
    """Evaluate ONE whitelisted-AST arithmetic expression (reuses tradeoff_modeler's validator +
    interpreter). Deterministic, no LLM, no sandbox. EXPLORATORY: the result carries no evidence
    span, so it is planner-facing (``renderable=False``) — to RENDER a derived number use
    verified_compute."""
    from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.tradeoff_modeler import (  # noqa: PLC0415
        _eval_formula, _formula_names,
    )

try:
        maybe = fetcher(u, cap)
        row = await maybe if asyncio.iscoroutine(maybe) else maybe
    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — a single fetch must not crash the loop
        return ToolResult(success=False, tool_name="fetch_url",
                          markdown=f"fetch_url failed for {u}: {exc}", error=str(exc)[:300])
    if not isinstance(row, dict):
        return ToolResult(success=False, tool_name="fetch_url",
                          markdown=f"fetch_url got no usable content from {u}.", error="empty_fetch")

    fold = await fold_in_fetched_rows(
        workspace, [row], research_question=workspace.research_question, agent_model=agent_model,
    )
    disclosure = (
        f"fetch_url[{u}] kept {fold.n_kept}, url-dup {fold.n_url_dup}, deleted {fold.n_deleted} "
        f"(chrome={len(fold.deleted_chrome)}, off-topic={len(fold.deleted_offtopic)})"
    )
    workspace.disclose(disclosure)
    kept_ids = [r.get("evidence_id") for r in fold.kept_rows]
    md = [f"**{disclosure}**"]
    for eid in kept_ids:
        md.append(f"- {eid}: {u} [CITE:{eid}]")
    return ToolResult(
        success=fold.n_kept > 0, tool_name="fetch_url", markdown="\n".join(md),
        source_evidence_ids=[e for e in kept_ids if e],
        statistics={"kept": fold.n_kept, "url_dup": fold.n_url_dup, "deleted": fold.n_deleted},
    )


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# find_contradictions — surface conflict (direction / magnitude outlier), never average
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
_NUM_RE = re.compile(r"[-+]?\d[\d,]*(?:\.\d+)?")


def _headline_numbers(text: str) -> list[float]:
    """All parseable magnitudes in a row's text (commas stripped), skipping bare 4-digit years."""
    vals: list[float] = []
    for m in _NUM_RE.findall(text or ""):
        raw = m.replace(",", "")
        try:
            v = float(raw)
        except ValueError:
            continue
        # skip a bare calendar year (1900-2099) so a date is not mistaken for a magnitude
        if raw.isdigit() and 1900 <= v <= 2099 and "." not in raw:
            continue
        vals.append(v)
    return vals


async def _tool_find_contradictions(
    workspace: Any, *, ev_ids: Any = None, outlier_ratio: float = 100.0, **_ignored: Any,
) -> ToolResult:
    """Surface CONFLICT among a set of evidence rows on the same aspect — deterministic, spend-free.

    Two conflict modes (design Category D #26): (1) DIRECTION conflict — one row asserts an explicit
    increase and another an explicit decrease on the endpoint; (2) MAGNITUDE outlier — one row's
    headline value is >= ``outlier_ratio`` x another's (the 1000x-off poison outlier, H22). Conflicts
    are surfaced as named material with BOTH ev_ids; nothing is averaged and nothing is deleted
    (§-1.3 weight, don't filter). Read-only: never mutates ``ev_store``.

    NOTE: this is the deterministic core. Pairwise NLI-entailment via the mirror model is an
    OPTIONAL fail-open enrichment (not run here to stay spend-free); it can only ADD advisory
    conflicts, never suppress a deterministic one.
    """
    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.contradiction_detector import _extract_direction  # noqa: PLC0415

    ids = [str(e) for e in (ev_ids or list(workspace.ev_store.keys()))]
    rows = [(e, workspace.ev_store.get(e)) for e in ids]
    rows = [(e, r) for e, r in rows if isinstance(r, dict)]
    try:
        ratio = float(outlier_ratio)
    except (TypeError, ValueError):
        ratio = 100.0

    raw_feats = []
    for e, r in rows:
        text = _row_text(r)
        raw_feats.append((e, _extract_direction(text), _headline_numbers(text)))

    # Strip magnitudes that appear IDENTICALLY in every row (a shared denominator/unit such as the
    # "per 100,000" in a rate, or a common cohort size) — they are not distinguishing values and
    # would false-flag a magnitude outlier (rate vs its own denominator). Deterministic, order-free.
    shared: set[float] | None = None
    for _e, _d, nums in raw_feats:
        s = set(nums)
        shared = s if shared is None else (shared & s)
    shared = shared or set()
    feats = [
        (e, d, [n for n in nums if n not in shared] or list(nums))
        for e, d, nums in raw_feats
    ]

    pairs: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    involved: set[str] = set()
    for i in range(len(feats)):
        for j in range(i + 1, len(feats)):
            ei, di, ni = feats[i]
            ej, dj, nj = feats[j]
            if di and dj and di != dj:
                pairs.append({"a": ei, "b": ej, "type": "direction_conflict",
                              "direction": f"{ei}:{di} vs {ej}:{dj}"})
                involved.update((ei, ej))
                continue
            if ni and nj:
                hi, lo = max(ni), min(nj)
                hi2, lo2 = max(nj), min(ni)
                best = max(
                    (hi / lo if lo else float("inf")),
                    (hi2 / lo2 if lo2 else float("inf")),
                )
                if best >= ratio:
                    pairs.append({"a": ei, "b": ej, "type": "magnitude_outlier",
                                  "ratio": round(best, 2)})
                    involved.update((ei, ej))

    if not pairs:
        return ToolResult(
            success=True, tool_name="find_contradictions",
            markdown=f"No direction/magnitude conflict among {len(rows)} row(s) "
                     "(agreement or unresolved — never silently averaged).",
            statistics={"conflicts": 0, "rows": len(rows), "pairs": []},
        )
    lines = [f"**find_contradictions: {len(pairs)} conflict pair(s)** across {len(rows)} rows", ""]
    for p in pairs:
        if p["type"] == "direction_conflict":
            lines.append(f"- DIRECTION CONFLICT {p['a']} <-> {p['b']}: {p['direction']} "
                         f"[CITE:{p['a']}] [CITE:{p['b']}]")
        else:
            lines.append(f"- MAGNITUDE OUTLIER {p['a']} <-> {p['b']}: {p['ratio']}x apart "
                         f"[CITE:{p['a']}] [CITE:{p['b']}]")
    lines.append("")
    lines.append("These are CONFLICTING-evidence material (surface both sides; never average, "
                 "never delete — §-1.3 weight, don't filter).")
    return ToolResult(
        success=True, tool_name="find_contradictions", markdown="\n".join(lines),
        source_evidence_ids=sorted(involved),
        statistics={"conflicts": len(pairs), "rows": len(rows), "pairs": pairs},
    )

seams.concern: evidence row schema (the canonical row builder — what metadata EXISTS)
seams.lines: 7566-7835 (base dict 7566-7597; conditional stamps 7617-7834; append 7835). Zero-weight fetch-failed variant: 7866-7888
seams.what_it_does: Builds EVERY groundable evidence row. Base keys: evidence_id (f"ev_{i:03d}"), source_url, statement (= cand.title[:300] — NOT a claim, a TITLE), title, direct_quote (a _build_provenance_quote window over cleaned body), tier (T1-T7), source (serper/s2), full_content_length, query_origin (the raw sub-query STRING that surfaced the row). Conditional stamps: document_type, content_relevance_label/weight, year/publication_year/pub_date, is_retracted, identity_consistent/source_title, relevance_weight, retrieval_weight/down_weighted/content_starved/landing_page/citation_metadata_shell/full_text_capable, wrong_content_span/fetch_degraded, authority_score/authority_confidence.
seams.how_it_blocks_synthesis: This is the hard ceiling on any comparison matrix. VERIFIED against the real corpus (/workspace/s2s3_wt/outputs/s2s3_iter1/s2/cp2_corpus_snapshot.json, 654 rows, the drb_72 AI-labor run): the ONLY fields on 654/654 rows are direct_quote, evidence_id, source_url, statement, tier, title, topic_offtopic_demoted, line_screen. 649/654 add content_relevance_label/weight, document_type, full_content_length, identity_consistent, query_origin, relevance_weight, selection_relevance, source, title_source. There is NO technology field, NO unit-of-analysis, NO outcome, NO method/study-design, NO industry/sector, NO geography, NO sample size, NO effect size, NO comparator — not one of them, anywhere in the row. Worse: `year` is present on only 54/654 rows (8%) and pub_date on 49; authors/doi/journal/pmid exist on exactly 5 rows (the hand-seeded v30 frame rows). `document_type` is UNKNOWN on 464/654 and `tier` is UNKNOWN on 165/654. EVERY axis of a comparison matrix would have to be DERIVED from the free text of `direct_quote` — nothing is stored. An interpretive sentence has no structured substrate to be interpretive ABOUT.

seams.concern: strict_verify gate — the single reason interpretive prose cannot ship
seams.lines: verify_sentence_provenance 2098-2500+ (no-token drop 2186-2193; content-overlap floor 2458-2461; binding_qualifier_dropped 2410-2427; entailment judge 2550-2565). strict_verify 3598-3818. MIN_CONTENT_WORD_OVERLAP=2 at 1339-1341.
seams.what_it_does: Sentence-level gate. (1) `if not tokens: failure_reasons=["no_provenance_token"]` — a sentence with no [#ev:id:start-end] token is dropped OUTRIGHT (2186-2193). (2) Every number in the sentence must appear verbatim in the union of cited spans (`number_not_in_any_cited_span`, 2306). (3) >= MIN_CONTENT_WORD_OVERLAP (default 2, env PG_PROVENANCE_MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP) content words shared with the cited span text (2458-2461). (4) NLI entailment judge, PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT default **enforce** (2553-2556) — NEUTRAL or CONTRADICTED drops the sentence. Kept sentences only.
seams.how_it_blocks_synthesis: "This discrepancy may reflect adoption lags" does NOT survive. Two independent kills: (a) it is a cross-source INFERENCE, so no single evidence row's direct_quote contains it — the writer either emits it with no [ev_XXX] marker (→ `no_provenance_token`, dropped) or attaches a marker, in which case _rewrite_draft_with_spans binds it to a span and the NLI judge in enforce mode returns NEUTRAL (the span does not entail 'may reflect adoption lags') → dropped. (b) Even the lenient path fails: 'discrepancy/reflect/adoption/lags' would need >=2 of those content words to literally appear in the ONE cited span. The ONLY prose in the whole report that escapes this is the 'Limitations:' paragraph, which is explicit pass-through (3792-3800: `kept.append(SentenceVerification(is_verified=True, soft_warnings=["limitations_paragraph_pass_through"]))`) unless a telemetry_block is supplied. So the system's design position is: interpretation is not a claim, and only claims can ship.

seams.concern: writer prompt — what the writer actually sees per row
seams.how_it_blocks_synthesis: The writer is structurally incapable of building a comparison matrix because it is never shown a comparable axis. Note the bitter irony at multi_section_generator.py:3619-3621 (prompt rules M-32/M-38/M-42a): the prompt repeatedly says 'if the evidence row carries this structured metadata, you MUST emit N / baseline / comparator / endpoint / timepoint' — the row NEVER carries it. The writer must re-extract every frame element from the free-text direct_quote on every call. The prompt is writing a cheque the schema cannot cash.

seams.concern: outline/menu — the digest line is the planner's entire view of a row
seams.what_it_does: Renders the outline LLM's MENU. A singleton line is literally `ev_003 [T4][w:demoted] | title: <title> | <statement>`. A basket line is `b12 [x4 works (7 rows): T1,T3] claim: "<200c claim>" members: ev_1,ev_9,...`. Tags available: [w:demoted] (_is_weight_demoted, 465-473), [chrome] (_is_chrome_interstitial, 444-454), [seminal T1]. Menu is capped at PG_OUTLINE_MAX_EV, default 150 (multi_section_generator.py:890).
seams.how_it_blocks_synthesis: The planner picks ev_ids from ID + tier + title + a 160-char statement + a corroboration count. It cannot group rows by technology/method/geography because those strings do not exist on the line. Any comparison-matrix outline (e.g. a section that contrasts manufacturing-sector studies against services-sector studies) would have to be produced by the LLM reading titles and guessing — which is exactly what the code comment at line 871 admits happened before titles were carried forward ('the outliner ... placed sources by tier marker alone ("ev_022 [T2]") and admitted guessing', live_retriever.py:7551-7566).

seams.concern: evidence assignment — how ev_ids get onto sections
seams.what_it_does: Assignment happens in exactly TWO ways. (1) The outline LLM emits `"ev_ids": ["ev_001", ...]` per section, parsed by _parse_outline (multi_section_generator.py:1773; prompt contract at 1587, 1628, 1664, 1699) — the LLM's pick from the digest menu. (2) Every basket/singleton NOT claimed by any section is routed by BAG-OF-WORDS OVERLAP between the basket's content words and the section title+focus words (`best_overlap`, ties → section order; overlap 0 → a keep-all residual section). SectionPlan (multi_section_generator.py:1252-1275) = {title, focus, ev_ids, archetype, undersupplied, basket_ids}.
seams.how_it_blocks_synthesis: There is no semantic assignment layer at all — no facet classifier stamps a row with a comparison dimension. The nearest thing on a row is `query_origin`, which is the raw sub-query STRING that retrieved it (evidence_selector.py:1273-1275, `_row_query_origin`), used only for per-sub-topic diversity reservation (_reserve_subqueries, 1303). Also note `schemas.py:1545-1606` defines SectionEvidenceAssignment/GlobalEvidenceAssignment (the 'two-pass global evidence assignment' the task asked about) — but grep shows the ONLY consumer is src/polaris_graph/agents/synthesizer.py:200/250, the legacy LangGraph pipeline. The flywheel run path (real_corpus_run.py → outline_agent → multi_section_generator → verified_compose) NEVER uses it. That code is dead for this pipeline.

seams.concern: topic judge dispositions — the only per-row semantic verdict, and it is a scalar
seams.what_it_does: Batches (index, title, snippet) to an LLM and parses ONE line per source: `<i>: ON` | `<i>: OFF_ASPECT` | `<i>: OFF_SUBJECT` (or legacy ON/OFF). OFF_ASPECT = same subject wrong aspect → KEPT + demoted. OFF_SUBJECT = different subject → the only deletable verdict. Result stamps `topic_offtopic_demoted` / `topic_off_subject` on the row (measured: 162/654 rows carry topic_off_subject=True); the W2 sibling stamps content_relevance_label ∈ {relevant, demoted, escalated_relevant, escalated_demoted, wall_rescue_floor} (measured distribution: 386 demoted, 90 relevant, 65 escalated_demoted, 57 wall_rescue_floor, 51 escalated_relevant).
seams.how_it_blocks_synthesis: The judge answers 'is this row on-topic?' — a 1-D relevance scalar. It does NOT answer 'what is this row ABOUT (which technology / which sector / which outcome)'. It is prompted to reason about a subject entity and an aspect internally, but the OUTPUT CONTRACT is explicitly verdict-only: 'Do NOT write the entity or aspect names, any reasoning, any explanation' (lines 300-306). The aspect it computed is thrown away. That is the single cheapest place to harvest comparison-matrix metadata and the prompt deliberately discards it.

seams.concern: cross-source synthesis — the most 'interpretive' pass in the codebase, and it is a consolidator
seams.what_it_does: Takes ONE basket (rows asserting the SAME claim, >=2 corroborating sources) and asks the generator model to write ONE consolidated sentence, then RE-GROUNDS it through the unchanged strict_verify against a BASKET-ID-SCOPED pool. System prompt: 'You NEVER add a fact, number, or claim that is not in a provided span.'
seams.how_it_blocks_synthesis: This is the ceiling on synthesis and it is deliberately low. It operates WITHIN a basket (same-claim rows), so it can say 'four sources agree X' or 'sources are in tension on X' — it can NEVER compare TWO DIFFERENT findings, because a sentence citing a different basket's evidence is absent from the scoped verify pool and 'fails CLOSED' (docstring line 23-24). 'This discrepancy may reflect adoption lags' is a cross-BASKET inference and is structurally unreachable: there is no code path in which a writer is given two contrasting findings and asked to explain the gap.

seams.concern: the existing comparison matrix — proof that matrix axes are DERIVED by keyword lexicon, not stored
seams.lines: module docstring 1-36 (the faithfulness contract); geography lexicon ~120-260; cell build 1134-1136; _cell_for_role 1242; GAP_CELL = '—' at 61; _MAX_TERMS_PER_CELL = 3 at 65
seams.what_it_does: The pipeline ALREADY builds a comparison matrix — the drb_72 summary table with columns Research Literature | Country/Region | Application Area/Occupation | Specific Applications and Impacts | Key Risks. One row per cited source. The Country/Region cell is produced by `_match_geography(span_blob)` matching a HARDCODED ~200-entry ("germany", "Germany"), ("brazilian", "Brazil") lexicon VERBATIM against the source's verified spans. Application-area and risk cells likewise via _DOMAIN_PHRASES / _RISK_PHRASES. No verbatim match → the cell renders an em dash: 'an honest DISCLOSED GAP; nothing is inferred or invented' (docstring line 30).
seams.how_it_blocks_synthesis: This is the exact shape of the answer to the question asked. Geography — the one comparison axis POLARIS actually renders — is not a row field; it is derived at RENDER time by literal string matching over the verified span, capped at 3 terms, and left blank when it does not match. Every other axis (technology, unit of analysis, outcome, method, industry) has no lexicon and no cell. Building a real comparison matrix means either (a) writing five more hardcoded lexicons in this file, or (b) adding a real extraction pass that stamps the axes onto the evidence row at ingest — which does not exist today. And even a filled matrix cannot be COMMENTED on: any sentence explaining a pattern across matrix rows dies in strict_verify.

THINGS THAT DO NOT EXIST (stated plainly, as requested):
- technology / intervention: DOES NOT EXIST on the row.
- unit of analysis (worker / firm / occupation / task / country): DOES NOT EXIST.
- outcome / endpoint / effect size / direction: DOES NOT EXIST.
- method / study design (RCT, diff-in-diff, survey, simulation): DOES NOT EXIST. `document_type` is the closest and it is UNKNOWN on 464/654 rows; its values are genre (REPORT / PREPRINT / JOURNAL_ARTICLE / BLOG_COMMENTARY), not method.
- industry / sector / occupation: DOES NOT EXIST.
- geography: DOES NOT EXIST on the row. It is derived at RENDER time by a hardcoded keyword lexicon in summary_table.py, per-cell, em-dash on miss.
- sample size / N / baseline / comparator: DOES NOT EXIST — despite multi_section_generator.py:3619-3621 instructing the writer to emit them "if the evidence row carries this structured metadata".
- year: EXISTS but is present on only 8% of rows, so even a time axis is mostly unusable without a backfill.
- GlobalEvidenceAssignment / SectionEvidenceAssignment (schemas.py:1545-1606) EXIST but are DEAD CODE on this pipeline — sole consumer is the legacy agents/synthesizer.py:200,250 LangGraph path, not the flywheel path.

THE ONE PLACE STRUCTURE ALMOST EXISTS: evidence_distiller.DistilledFinding (evidence_distiller.py:227-240) = {finding_id, evidence_id, claim, span_start/end, support_quote, numbers[], entities[], caveat, contradiction_key, source_tier}. The MAP prompt (370-396) already asks the LLM for `entities` and `numbers` per finding. But SectionDistillate (258-264) is built PER SECTION inside the compose call and thrown away — it is never written back to evidence_pool, so no other section and no later stage can see it. That is the cheapest existing hook for a comparison matrix: `entities` is already extracted and already discarded.

SECOND-CHEAPEST HOOK: the topic judge (topic_relevance_gate.py:281-306) internally reasons about "subject entity" and "aspect" per row — then its OUTPUT CONTRACT explicitly forbids writing them down ("Do NOT write the entity or aspect names"). It already visits every row with an LLM. It already computes the axis. It throws it away.

THIRD: contradiction_detector.extract_numeric_claims produces (subject, predicate, value, unit, evidence_id, source_tier) tuples by regex — the ONLY structured, comparable representation in the system. Computed at detect time from direct_quote, consumed for contradiction disclosure, never persisted on the row.

DOES AN INTERPRETIVE SENTENCE SURVIVE? No — and the block is by explicit design, in three redundant layers:
1. strict_verify drops any sentence with no [#ev:] token (provenance_generator.py:2186-2193, `no_provenance_token`).
2. A sentence WITH a token must share >=2 content words with the cited span (2458-2461) AND pass an NLI entailment judge whose default mode is **enforce** (2553-2556) — an inference over two sources is NEUTRAL w.r.t. either span and drops.
3. The one synthesis pass that could plausibly interpret (depth_synthesis) is scoped to a SINGLE basket (same-claim rows) with a verify pool that "fails CLOSED" on a token from another basket, and its system prompt is "You NEVER add a fact, number, or claim that is not in a provided span."
The sole escape hatch is the "Limitations:" paragraph (provenance_generator.py:3792-3800), which is pass-through and un-verified. So today the ONLY place POLARIS can write an interpretive sentence is the one paragraph where it disclaims its own pipeline.

Orphan routing is bag-of-words: verified_compose.py:3863 `overlap = len(bw & pw)` over section title+focus words. That is the entire semantics of "which section does this evidence belong to" for any row the outline LLM did not hand-pick.
